Practice-Wide Performance claim file
Top-line revenue, collections, and patient activity. Totals now reflect the broader claim-level export — $35.7M billed across the full patient population, including facilities not present in the original CPT export.

Production vs Collections claim

Monthly billed charges against collected payments

Revenue by Specialty claim

Share of collections across active entities

Revenue by Provider × Month claim

Stacked monthly collections, one band per provider

Provider Productivity claim

Total collections per provider
Revenue Growth — MoM & YoY
MonthProductionCollectionsMoMYoYUnique PatientsCollection %
Patient Analytics claim file
Patient-level analytics enabled by the new claim-level export, which carries patient names — allowing identification of unique patients, new-patient cohorts (by first appearance in data), and revenue concentration.

New Patient Acquisition by Month

Patients whose first-ever recorded visit falls in each month, split by first-specialty

Patient Revenue Distribution (Pareto)

Cumulative revenue share as patients are ranked from highest to lowest revenue

Visit Frequency Distribution

How many claims per unique patient

Patient Entry Specialty Mix

Which entity first acquires the patient
Top 25 Patients by Revenue
Highest-revenue patients in the filtered range. The top decile often drives a disproportionate share of collections.
#PatientFirst VisitLast VisitVisitsRevenueSpecialties Seen
Cross-Specialty Patient Flow claim file
Patients who appear at more than one of GVV / GFA / GH across the full data history. Flow direction is determined by which specialty saw the patient first.
Now computable. This metric was blocked in v1 because the original CPT export had no patient identifier. The claim file's Patient Name column makes it possible to track patients across facilities.

Specialty Flow Diagram

Arrows show the count of patients whose first specialty visit was on the left, and who later attended a specialty on the right. Computed across the full data history regardless of date filter (a referral may have happened before the window).

Referral Counts by Pair

Conversion Rates

Of patients first seen at a specialty, the % who eventually visit another specialty
Cross-Specialty Patient Roster
All patients with visits in two or more active specialties.
PatientSpecialtiesFirst SpecLast VisitTotal VisitsTotal Revenue
Gauld Vein & Vascular — Monthly Patient Numbers CPT file
All metrics by location (Buc / Mar / EC). Driven by the CPT-level export which carries CPT codes. Heads-up: CPT 99205 not in export — only 99203 and 99204 contribute to New Patient counts.

Data Quality & Source Lineage
Where each metric comes from, what's been excluded, and what to confirm with the billing team.
v2 changes from v1: Added claim-level export (Patient Name) which unlocked Patient Analytics and Referral Flow tabs. Added new CPT export which extends GFA-specific code coverage and introduces 3 new facilities (Atlanta Center for Reconstructive Foot & Ankle, Marietta Surgical Center, Sterling Estates of West Cobb).
Source files
SourceFileRecordsDate RangeFeeds these metrics
CLAIM 36.1436.14 Financial Analysis at Claim Level11,485Apr 2023 – Sep 2025Executive KPIs, Patient Analytics, Referral Flow, Revenue Growth, Provider productivity
CPT 37.08 v137.08 Financial Analysis at CPT Level (v1)2,192Nov 2023 – May 2026GVV / GFA / GH metric reports, Procedure Mix, all CPT-specific rollups
CPT 37.08 v237.08 Financial Analysis at CPT Level (v2 supplement)355Aug 2023 – Apr 2026Supplemental — 20 GFA-specific codes including new facilities
Excluded / flagged data
IssueDetailDefault handling
OLD - DO NOT USE facilities"GFA Marietta OLD - DO NOT USE" (3,119 claim rows) + "GVV Marietta OLD - DO NOT USE" (1,515 claim rows). Likely deprecated facility records.Excluded from active reporting; toggle in filter bar to include.
CPT 99205 absentHighest-level new-patient E&M code has zero rows. Either not billed or filtered out of export.New Patient totals include 99203 + 99204 only. Confirm with billing.
CPT FTPIN absentFat Pad injection code not present in any export.Rollup table flags as "missing". Confirm code name with billing.
Atlanta Center for Reconstructive Foot & Ankle17 claim rows; not part of the original 3-entity model.Mapped to "ACRFA" specialty group; excluded from GVV/GFA/GH-specific tabs.
Marietta Surgical Center13 claim rows; a separate facility, possibly surgical suite for procedures.Mapped to "MSC" specialty group; excluded from GVV/GFA/GH-specific tabs.
Sterling Estates of West Cobb4 claim rows; senior care facility, likely Gauld Health outreach.Mapped to "STER" specialty group; consider re-mapping to GH.
Online Payments GH2 claim rows; payment-only entries with no clinical service.Treated as GH for now; consider excluding from visit counts.
Change in A/R is delta onlyOpening A/R balance not in either export.Cumulative A/R anchored to zero at first month in range.

Metricv1 Statusv2 StatusEnabled by
Internal Referral ConversionBlocked — no patient IDComputedPatient Name column in claim file

Patient-level revenue distributionNot possibleComputedPatient Name + Payments
Avg revenue per patientNot possibleComputedPatient Name aggregation
Visits per patient (retention proxy)Not possibleComputedClaim Count grouped by patient
Case AcceptanceBlockedStill blockedNeeds treatment-plan data from EHR
Patient Acquisition CostBlockedPartially unblockedNow has new-patient denominator; still needs marketing spend
Google ReviewsExternalStill externalNeeds Google Business Profile API
